summary: Backend regression for Lumi's new features: Stripe Checkout (DEMO mode fallback) + Build-an-outfit multi-product AI styling. 23/23 pytest tests passed (100%) against the public preview URL. Covered: auth protection on 8 new endpoints, empty cart 400, demo session creation (session_id starts with 'demo_', demo_mode=true, order persisted as pending), verify endpoint marks paid+clears cart+records purchase events+completes order, invalid demo session 404, cross-user access returns 404 (acceptable per spec), orders sorted desc, outfit AI styling success+empty(400)+invalid ids(404), outfit save/list-enriched/add-to-cart/delete with 404 on bad id.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1215 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth, products, cart, wishlist, beauty, stylist, stores, checkout, outfits) before adding more features.

STRIPE_API_KEY in backend/.env is 'sk_test_emergent' (placeholder). DEMO fallback in /api/checkout/session correctly catches stripe.error.AuthenticationError and returns a demo session. Real Stripe path is unexercisable until a real test key is provisioned — MOCKED behavior is INTENTIONAL per problem statement.

GET /api/checkout/session/{id} for a non-owner of a demo_ session returns 404 (because order lookup is scoped by user_id). Spec explicitly allows this. The real-Stripe branch returns 403 instead — not testable here.

POST /api/outfits/{id}/add-to-cart silently picks the first size/color from arrays via p.get('sizes', [None])[0]; if a product has empty sizes list this could be None which is fine, but if sizes is [] it would IndexError. Spot-checked seed data — all products have non-empty sizes/colors arrays so OK in practice.
Demo-mode verify endpoint re-runs the 'clear cart + purchase events' block only if order.status != 'completed' (idempotent on repeat calls) — good.
/api/orders does not paginate (cap 50). Acceptable for current scope.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_checkout_outfits.py (new — 23 pytest cases covering checkout, orders, outfits, and auth protection)
seed_data_creation: Each test creates its own fresh user via /api/auth/signup (TEST_ prefix). Cart items + outfits + orders scoped to each test user; no shared state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_checkout_outfits.py -v. Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env. Stripe demo fallback is INTENTIONAL — tests assert demo_mode=true and session_id starts with 'demo_'. The outfit styling test calls Claude Sonnet 4.5 (~5-10s). Full suite runs in ~22s.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 14 features in the review request behave as specified, including the DEMO Stripe fallback path.
